source_reviews.notes: The report supports 695 announced 2025 deals and the named buyers and counts used in the narrative. It supports at least seven large active buyers, while its fuller table names additional buyers above the selected threshold. It does not measure owner age, so the 0.30 owner-age input remains the disclosed estimate rather than an observed statistic.

The page supports the survey population, the 23%/39%/29% expectation shares, integration into current systems, and expected help in data, reporting, back-office, rating, quoting, and underwriting workflows. It does not directly measure effective current adoption, time to 50% deployment, historical AMS/cloud diffusion, or the selected role-automation fractions; those are explicitly estimated with ranges and caveats.

NAIC supports the requirement that people selling, soliciting, or negotiating insurance be licensed as producers. It does not quantify the selected automation, distribution-capture, moat, commission-mix, or recurring-revenue inputs; those are estimates, and the record discloses direct-distribution and unmeasured pricing-mix limits.

The page supports the 11.8x first-half-2025 average for insurance-broker deals with at least $1.0 million of EBITDA. It does not isolate the target $1-10 million retail P&C/benefits band or directly establish either selected 8.0x multiple; the record discloses that scope mismatch and uses S4 only as broader market context.

The article supports a generic insurance-agency EBITDA x8 ballpark, a conservative 7x-11x sensitivity range, and the statement that more than 90% of observed agency deals use an EBITDA multiple. It is a rough rule of thumb rather than target-specific transaction evidence and does not forecast an exit multiple; the record discloses the target-scope limitation and explicitly assumes no entry-to-exit re-rating.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions measure task replaceability, not realized layoffs or adoption speed.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Large residual wage bucket requires a bounded proxy judgment.
inputs.pi_dist: Commission schedules and fee-account repricing can pass savings through.
inputs.pi_moat: Licensing does not prevent carriers from going direct.
inputs.t50_years: Vendor survey captures expectations, not audited production use.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Not a direct prevalence measure; distinction between experimentation and effective deployment is judgmental.
inputs.historical_analogs: No quantified historical adoption series selected.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not an observed owner-age statistic and should not be treated as one.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Avoids treating consolidation as proof of succession shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: Counts named high-volume buyers, not every acquirer.
inputs.buy_mult: S4 includes all insurance-broker deals above $1M EBITDA and does not isolate the $1-10M retail P&C/benefits target band.
inputs.buy_mult: Use normalized EBITDA after contingent-commission, owner-dependency and retention diligence.
inputs.exit_mult: S4 includes all insurance-broker deals above $1M EBITDA and does not isolate the $1-10M retail P&C/benefits target band.
inputs.exit_mult: No multiple expansion is assumed between entry and exit.
inputs.pricing_structure: Commission percentages and account fee mix were not directly measured here.
summary: Mechanics, identity, model routing, digest binding, deterministic dataset injection, fresh finalization, fresh memo rendering, and arithmetic all pass. All five registered score-driving sources are accessible. They support the useful direction of the workflow, licensing, consolidation, and valuation evidence, while the selected adoption, automation, capture, ownership, target-specific multiple, and future-exit judgments remain disclosed estimates or broad proxies. Factor semantics are not reversed, and no terminal/capture or ownership/consolidation reuse is likely to change the thesis: the no-re-rating 8.0x entry/exit case sets M to zero and independently preserves the kill verdict. The record is therefore publishable with its fifteen authored caveats.
